findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "book_event" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Submit a request to book Rick Hebert for an event. This sends the details to Rick, who personally reviews every request and replies (usually within a day) to co…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: get_contact · Private data: book_event, get_contact · Public sink: book_event, get_contact · Destructive: list_packages.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: get_contact · Private data: book_event, get_contact · Public sink: book_event, get_contact · Destructive: list_packages

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
